result.finding: Assessed 6 CWE rules in Concurrency & Synchronization: 0 FOUND, 6 NOT_FOUND
evidence: Scanned application source files for concurrency & synchronization patterns

values.name: Use of Singleton Pattern Without Synchronization in a Multithreaded Context
values.description: The product uses the singleton pattern when creating a resource within a multithreaded environment.

values.name: Unsynchronized Access to Shared Data in a Multithreaded Context
values.description: The product does not properly synchronize shared data, such as static variables across threads, which can lead to undefined behavior and unpredictable data changes.

values.name: Improper Synchronization
values.description: The product utilizes multiple threads or processes to allow temporary access to a shared resource that can only be exclusive to one process at a time, but it does not properly synchronize these actions, which might cause simultaneous accesses of this resource by multiple threads or processes.

values.name: Improper Locking
values.description: The product does not properly acquire or release a lock on a resource, leading to unexpected resource state changes and behaviors.

values.name: Missing Synchronization
values.description: The product utilizes a shared resource in a concurrent manner but does not attempt to synchronize access to the resource.

values.name: Incorrect Synchronization
values.description: The product utilizes a shared resource in a concurrent manner, but it does not correctly synchronize access to the resource.
